heading: Safeguard exceptions

children.heading: Next business day availability of cash and certain items
children.label: 1 Next business day availability of cash and certain items
A
children.text: any cash deposited in such account;

children.label: B
children.text: any funds received by such depository institution by wire transfer for deposit in such account;

children.label: C
children.text: any funds deposited in such account by cashier’s check, certified check, teller’s check, depository check, or traveler’s check; and

children.label: D
children.text: any funds deposited by a government check which is described in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of ,section 4002(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Availability of other items
children.label: 2 Availability of other items
children.text: In the case of any funds deposited in such account by a check (other than a check described in subparagraph (C) or (D) of paragraph (1)), the availability for withdrawal of such funds shall not be subject to the provisions of section 4002(b), 4002(c), or paragraphs (1) of .1section 4002(e) of this titleSo in original. Probably should be “paragraph”.1

children.heading: Limitation relating to certain checks in excess of $5,000
children.label: 3 Limitation relating to certain checks in excess of $5,000
A
children.text: paragraph (1) shall apply only with respect to the first $5,000 of such aggregate amount; and

children.label: B
children.text: not more than 8 business days shall intervene between the business day on which any such funds are deposited and the business day on which such excess amount shall be available for withdrawal.

children.heading: Large or redeposited checks; repeated overdrafts
children.label: b Large or redeposited checks; repeated overdrafts

children.text: the amount of deposits by one or more checks that exceeds the amount of $5,000 in any one day;
checks that have been returned unpaid and redeposited; and
deposit accounts which have been overdrawn repeatedly.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In accordance with regulations which the Board, jointly with the Director of the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection, shall prescribe, subsections (a)(2), (b), (c), and (e) of shall not apply with respect to any check deposited in an account at a depository institution if the receiving depository institution has reasonable cause to believe that the check is uncollectible from the originating depository institution. For purposes of the preceding sentence, reasonable cause to believe requires the existence of facts which would cause a well-grounded belief in the mind of a reasonable person. Such reasons shall be included in the notice required under subsection (f).section 4002 of this title

children.heading: Basis for determination
children.label: 2 Basis for determination
children.text: No determination under this subsection may be based on any class of checks or persons.

children.label: A
children.text: the depositor was not provided with the written notice required under subsection (f) (with respect to such determination) at the time the deposit was made;

children.label: B
children.text: the overdraft would not have occurred but for the fact that the funds so deposited are not available; and

children.label: C
children.text: the amount of the check is collected from the originating depository institution.

children.heading: Compliance
children.label: 4 Compliance
children.text: Each agency referred to in shall monitor compliance with the requirements of this subsection in each regular examination of a depository institution and shall describe in each report to the Congress the extent to which this subsection is being complied with. For the purpose of this paragraph, each depository institution shall retain a record of each notice provided under subsection (f) as a result of the application of this subsection.section 4009(a) of this title
any interruption of communication facilities;
suspension of payments by another depository institution;
any war; or
any emergency condition beyond the control of the receiving depository institution,

children.label: A
children.text: depository institutions are experiencing an unacceptable level of losses due to check-related fraud, and

children.label: B
children.text: suspension of this chapter, or such portion of this chapter, with regard to the classification of checks involved in such fraud is necessary to diminish the volume of such fraud.

children.heading: Sunset provision
children.label: 2 Sunset provision
children.text: No regulation prescribed or order issued under paragraph (1) shall remain in effect for more than 45 days (excluding Saturdays, Sundays, legal holidays, or any day either House of Congress is not in session).

children.heading: Notice of each suspension
children.label: A Notice of each suspension
children.text: Within 10 days of prescribing any regulation or issuing any order under paragraph (1), the Board, jointly with the Director of the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection, shall transmit a report of such action to the Committee on Banking, Finance and Urban Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate.

children.label: i
children.text: the specific reason for prescribing the regulation or issuing the order;

children.label: ii
children.text: evidence considered by the Board, jointly with the Director of the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection, in making the determination under paragraph (1) with respect to such regulation or order; and

children.label: iii
children.text: specific examples of the check-related fraud giving rise to such regulation or order.

children.heading: Notice of exception; availability within reasonable time
children.label: f Notice of exception; availability within reasonable time
i
children.text: the time period within which the funds shall be made available for withdrawal; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the reason the exception was invoked; and

children.label: B
children.text: except where other time periods are specifically provided in this chapter, the availability of the funds deposited shall be governed by the policy of the receiving depository institution, but shall not exceed a reasonable period of time as determined by the Board, jointly with the Director of the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection.

children.label: A
children.text: In the case of a deposit made in person by the depositor at the receiving depository institution, the depository institution shall immediately provide such notice in writing to the depositor.

children.label: B
children.text: In the case of any other deposit (other than a deposit described in subparagraph (C)), the receiving depository institution shall mail the notice to the depositor not later than the close of the next business day following the business day on which the deposit is received.

children.label: C
children.text: In the case of a deposit to which subsection (d) or (e) applies, notice shall be provided by the depository institution in accordance with regulations of the Board, jointly with the Director of the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection.

children.label: D
children.text: In the case of a deposit to which subsection (b)(1) or (b)(2) applies, the depository institution may, for nonconsumer accounts and other classes of accounts, as defined by the Board, that generally have a large number of such deposits, provide notice at or before the time it first determines that the subsection applies.

children.label: E
children.text: In the case of a deposit to which subsection (b)(3) applies, the depository institution may, subject to regulations of the Board, provide notice at the beginning of each time period it determines that the subsection applies. In addition to the requirements contained in paragraph (1)(A), the notice shall specify the time period for which the exception will apply.

children.heading: Subsequent determinations
children.label: 3 Subsequent determinations
children.text: If the facts upon which the determination of the applicability of an exception contained in subsection (b) or (c) to any deposit only become known to the receiving depository institution after the time notice is required under paragraph (2) with respect to such deposit, the depository institution shall mail such notice to the depositor as soon as practicable, but not later than the first business day following the day such facts become known to the depository institution.
